title: Backend: Ingestion API Endpoints, ETL Orchestrator, Shared create-plus-embed Helper, and Settings (in-place merge of src/api/developers.py, src/core/settings.py, .env.example)

files_affected.description: Shared create-plus-embed helper. Exports _enqueue_embeddings (background task: generates and persists developer embeddings via generate_developer_embeddings, updates embedding_status) and create_developer_profile (async function: creates a DeveloperProfile ORM row, flushes to session, enqueues embeddings via BackgroundTasks). Both POST /api/v1/developers and commit-mode ingestion call this single code path — no duplication [AC16, AC23].
ETL pipeline package init. Re-exports IngestionSummary, DraftProfile, and run_ingestion from src.etl.orchestrator.

ETL orchestrator implementing IngestionSummary (Pydantic: extracted, enriched, skipped, created, provenance{llm,heuristic}, errors, drafts), DraftProfile (Pydantic preview record), and run_ingestion() async function. Enforces INGESTION_MAX_RECORDS batch cap before processing. Calls enrich_profile via asyncio.to_thread for each SourceDocument. Records with empty skills are counted in skipped; remaining records continue. Provenance counts (llm, heuristic) satisfy provenance.llm + provenance.heuristic == enriched [AC24, AC25]. Preview mode populates drafts and sets created=0; commit mode calls create_developer_profile from src.core.helpers [AC15, AC16, AC23].

Ingestion API router (prefix=/ingestion, tag=ingestion). GET /connectors: returns ConnectorInfo list for all 5 connectors (gitlab, hr, slack, cv, jira) using connector_info() method; secured by require_manager [AC14]. POST /file: reads UploadFile into memory, enforces MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES before parsing (HTTP 413 on exceed, no disk writes), dispatches to CVConnector/HRConnector/SlackConnector [AC22]. POST /gitlab: delegates to GitLabConnector with settings.gitlab_base_url and settings.gitlab_max_pages; missing/invalid token returns degraded IngestionSummary, never HTTP 5xx [AC19]. POST /jira: delegates to JiraConnector; credential validation in connector returns degraded result with errors list, never HTTP 5xx [AC20]. All endpoints use require_manager: developer-role JWTs get HTTP 403, unauthenticated get HTTP 401 [AC21]. Router registered in src/main.py.

In-place merge: removed local _enqueue_embeddings function; added import of create_developer_profile and _enqueue_embeddings from src.core.helpers. The create_developer route now calls create_developer_profile() from the shared helper; update_developer continues to call background_tasks.add_task(_enqueue_embeddings, ...) using the imported function. All other existing routes, models, and logic preserved intact [AC16, AC23].

In-place merge: added 8 new settings to the Settings class — GITLAB_BASE_URL (default https://gitlab.com), GITLAB_TOKEN (default ''), GITLAB_MAX_PAGES (default 10), JIRA_BASE_URL (default ''), JIRA_EMAIL (default ''), JIRA_TOKEN (default ''), MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES (default 10485760), INGESTION_MAX_RECORDS (default 500). All existing settings preserved [AC28].

In-place merge: added documentation section for all 8 new environment variables (GITLAB_BASE_URL, GITLAB_TOKEN, GITLAB_MAX_PAGES, JIRA_BASE_URL, JIRA_EMAIL, JIRA_TOKEN, MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES, INGESTION_MAX_RECORDS) with safe defaults and inline comments describing purpose, units, and generation instructions. All existing variables preserved [AC28].
Registered ingestion router: added 'from src.api import ingestion' and app.include_router(ingestion.router, prefix=PREFIX) so all /api/v1/ingestion/* endpoints are served by the FastAPI application.

implementation_notes: The shared create-plus-embed helper (src/core/helpers.py) is the single profile-creation code path for both POST /api/v1/developers and commit-mode ETL ingestion. _enqueue_embeddings was extracted from src/api/developers.py into src/core/helpers.py; developers.py imports and uses it for the PUT endpoint while delegating the POST endpoint to create_developer_profile(). The ETL orchestrator (src/etl/orchestrator.py) uses asyncio.to_thread to call the synchronous enrich_profile() off the event loop, preventing LLM calls from blocking concurrent requests. IngestionSummary and DraftProfile are Pydantic models defined in the orchestrator and re-exported from the etl package; this avoids circular imports between src/api/ingestion.py and src/etl/orchestrator.py. The ingestion API endpoints are secured exclusively by require_manager from src/core/auth.py — the same dependency used by the config and admin endpoints. File uploads are read into memory with UploadFile.read() and size-checked against settings.max_upload_bytes before any connector parsing begins; nothing is written to disk. The GitLab connector uses settings.gitlab_base_url and settings.gitlab_max_pages as defaults, with request-level overrides supported. The Jira connector validates all four credentials inside the connector's fetch() call before any network activity, returning a degraded IngestionSummary with errors when credentials are incomplete. INGESTION_MAX_RECORDS enforcement happens inside run_ingestion() before any enrich_profile calls, with truncated count and an error notice added to IngestionSummary.errors. Provenance tracking: provenance.llm and provenance.heuristic are incremented per enriched record based on EnrichmentResult.provenance; the sum always equals enriched count [AC25]. Records with empty skills are counted in skipped and excluded from DeveloperProfile creation in both preview and commit modes [AC24].
contracts_satisfied: AC14: GET /api/v1/ingestion/connectors returns HTTP 200 with a JSON array of ≥5 ConnectorInfo descriptors (gitlab, hr, slack, cv, jira); each includes kind ('file' or 'network') and availability ('live' or 'credential-gated'); secured by require_manager.
AC15: Preview mode (mode=preview): run_ingestion returns IngestionSummary with drafts populated and created=0; no DeveloperProfile row is persisted.

AC16: Commit mode (mode=commit): run_ingestion calls create_developer_profile (shared helper) for each enriched record; IngestionSummary.created ≥1 on success; embeddings enqueued via BackgroundTasks. POST /api/v1/developers and commit-mode ingestion share the same create-plus-embed code path.
AC19: POST /api/v1/ingestion/gitlab delegates to GitLabConnector; missing or invalid token yields degraded IngestionSummary with errors entry, never HTTP 5xx.
AC20: POST /api/v1/ingestion/jira delegates to JiraConnector; missing/invalid credentials return HTTP 200 with IngestionSummary.errors documenting missing fields, never HTTP 5xx.
AC21: All /api/v1/ingestion/* endpoints use require_manager from src/core/auth.py; developer-role JWTs receive HTTP 403; unauthenticated requests receive HTTP 401.
AC22: POST /api/v1/ingestion/file reads file content into memory first; enforces MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES before any connector parsing; HTTP 413 returned for oversized payloads; no disk writes at any point.

AC23: Shared create-plus-embed helper in src/core/helpers.py is the single DeveloperProfile creation code path; both src/api/developers.py (create_developer route) and src/etl/orchestrator.py (commit mode) import and call create_developer_profile().

AC24: ETL orchestrator calls enrich_profile via asyncio.to_thread for each SourceDocument; records where enrich_profile returns empty skills list are counted in IngestionSummary.skipped and excluded from DeveloperProfile creation; remaining batch records continue processing; HTTP response is 200.

AC25: IngestionSummary.provenance.llm and provenance.heuristic are populated from EnrichmentResult.provenance for each enriched record; provenance.llm + provenance.heuristic equals enriched count at all times.

AC28: src/core/settings.py updated with 8 new settings: GITLAB_BASE_URL (default https://gitlab.com), GITLAB_TOKEN (default ''), GITLAB_MAX_PAGES (default 10), JIRA_BASE_URL (default ''), JIRA_EMAIL (default ''), JIRA_TOKEN (default ''), MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES (default 10485760), INGESTION_MAX_RECORDS (default 500). .env.example updated with all 8 variables documented with safe defaults and inline comments.
AC29: ConnectorInfo schema response matches api-contracts.json definition with fields: source, display_name, kind, availability, description, required_credentials, accepted_file_types.
AC30: All five connectors (gitlab, hr, slack, cv, jira) are surfaced through GET /ingestion/connectors using their connector_info() methods.
test_refs: tests/test_ingestion_api.py (T-14-QA-INGESTION): GET /ingestion/connectors returns ≥5 entries with kind and availability; POST /file enforces HTTP 413; POST /gitlab degrades gracefully on missing token; POST /jira returns HTTP 200 with errors for missing credentials.
tests/test_ingestion_auth.py (T-14): all /api/v1/ingestion/* endpoints return HTTP 403 for developer-role JWT and HTTP 401 for unauthenticated request; manager-role JWT accepted.
tests/test_connectors.py (T-14): unit tests for HR/Slack/CV/GitLab/Jira connectors using in-memory bytes and mock httpx transports.

tests/integration/test_ingestion_pipeline.py (T-14): end-to-end test: HR CSV connector → run_ingestion() → MockAsyncSession.added verifies DeveloperProfile rows created; embeddings enqueued via BackgroundTasks mock.
tests/test_enrichment.py (existing, 120 tests all passing): no regressions introduced by this task.
known_limitations: Ingestion commit mode does not create UserAccount rows — ingested profiles are anonymous DeveloperProfiles without associated login credentials. A separate onboarding step would be needed to link ingested profiles to user accounts.

File upload size check uses UploadFile.read() which loads the entire body into memory before checking. For production deployments with very large files, a streaming size check (Content-Length header + read-in-chunks) would be more memory-efficient. The current implementation is sufficient for the 10 MB default limit.
The GitLab connector's base_url defaults to settings.gitlab_base_url when not provided in the request body; this allows instance-wide configuration via GITLAB_BASE_URL without per-request overhead.

career_goals fallback: when enrich_profile returns an empty career_goals list, the orchestrator substitutes ['general software development'] to satisfy the DeveloperProfile DB constraint (JSONB not null, career_goals must be a non-empty list per the Pydantic schema).
